title: ranunculus nevensis
intro: Ranunculus nevensis, also known as Neves' buttercup, is a flowering plant species within the family Ranunculaceae. It is native to Scotland and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting infections, due to its high content of alkaloids and glycosides. Ranunculus nevensis can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
